landscape_summary: The story this week is fragility at the top. MyFitnessPal's April 2026 'Today/Progress' redesign continued to bleed users into early May — Piunikaweb's May 5 follow-up reported ongoing cancellations and refund requests, and the company stated the new layout 'is here to stay,' triggering a workaround community that resets the app's region to Turkey to recover the older design. Lose It pulled macro tracking behind the Premium paywall (joining barcode scan there) — a continuation of the freemium-tightening pattern. Lifesum shipped Siri voice commands on iOS. Yazio shipped only minor maintenance updates (April 30 / May 7). Outside the big five: 'Simply' (AI nutrition tips by certified nutritionists) cracked Product Hunt's top 5 of the day. Two structural shifts deserve our attention this fortnight: (1) Wegovy is now formally launched in Israel — Times of Israel confirms the Health Ministry's directive ending off-label Ozempic prescriptions, with Wegovy out-of-basket at NIS 600–1,300/month, creating a defined private-pay cohort with no Hebrew-native companion app. (2) Ultra-processed-food (UPF) regulation is moving from advocacy to law — California's AB 1264 is signed, a US bipartisan bill is in motion, and Haaretz ran a UPF explainer on May 3, 2026 — pushing UPF labeling into mainstream Israeli discourse. Meanwhile CGM-for-non-diabetics keeps consolidating as a category (Dexcom Stelo OTC review in Athletech, Vively CGM-app retrospective study published in JMIR Human Factors 2026), but for our user segment this remains adjacent rather than core.

bullets: Redesign backlash extended into May (Piunikaweb, May 5, 2026): cancellations and refund requests continuing; company confirmed there is no revert option.

User-discovered workaround circulating: set account location to Turkey to restore the pre-redesign layout — a clear sign that core retention users still value the old IA over the new Today/Progress tabs.
GLP-1 tracking module is now available to free-tier users (no subscription required) — moves GLP-1 from a paid feature to a top-of-funnel acquisition hook.
Maintenance-only window: Play Store updates on April 30 and May 7 with the generic 'further improvements' release notes — no new feature announcement this fortnight.
AI photo logging (PRO) remains the lead marketing surface; portion-size estimation still flagged as needing manual correction in third-party reviews.
Macro tracking moved from free → Premium (per NutriScan and FitBudd 2026 reviews) — joins the barcode scanner behind the paywall. Free-tier value is being deliberately thinned.
iOS 26 'Liquid Glass' redesign live: leaner tab bar, 'Day Complete' moved beneath meals — small UX wins, shipping pace is steady.
Search experience redesigned with more whitespace and one-tap Quick Add on previous meals.
AI Food Logging + Welli (Noom's LLM assistant) now available to most members — photo/text/voice logging now in the mainstream Noom app rather than experimental.
Future Me (AI selfie aging) and Body Scan remain free-tier acquisition hooks; Seeds rewards (real-world redemption for steps) added.
GLP-1 Companion remains a free add-on to the paid program; medical pathway (clinician → prescription) is now a default route in Noom Med.
New Siri commands shipped: voice-driven access to multiple parts of the app from iOS — extends the multimodal-tracking stance into ambient/voice-first interaction.
Life Score algorithm and visual treatment refreshed; week-over-week change is now easier to read.

trends.trend: Incumbent fragility — UI stability as a competitive moat
trends.evidence: MyFitnessPal's April redesign continues to bleed users in May 2026 (Piunikaweb May 5); a Turkey-region workaround is being shared in user communities; Lose It is actively narrowing its free tier (macros now Premium); MyFitnessPal alternatives lists are republishing weekly.
trends.relevance: Israeli users who adopted MFP years ago and tolerate its English-only food DB are right now exposed to a redesign they did not ask for. This is a 4–8 week acquisition window where 'I just want my old diary back, in Hebrew' is a marketable proposition. We do not need to out-feature MFP; we need to be conspicuously stable, Hebrew-native, and trivially easy to switch into.

trends.evidence: Wegovy approved and launched in Israel, ending off-label Ozempic prescriptions for weight loss (Times of Israel); private-pay only, NIS 600–1,300/month; NPR (Jan 2026) and ScienceDaily (May 2 + May 4, 2026) document continued GLP-1 evidence accumulation, including links to lower depression/anxiety risk and side-effect cost trade-offs. MyFitnessPal made GLP-1 tracking free-tier; Noom keeps GLP-1 Companion free.
trends.relevance: An Israeli private-pay Wegovy patient is a high-intent, high-LTV user with specific nutrition needs (protein floors, micro-portion satiety, side-effect-aware meal swaps). No incumbent ships a Hebrew-native module for this cohort. This is where our Israeli context compounds GLP-1 expertise into a defensible niche.

trends.trend: Ultra-processed food (UPF) labeling moving from policy to product
trends.evidence: California AB 1264 signed (phasing UPF out of school meals); US bipartisan bill proposing FDA front-of-pack UPF warnings; FoodNavigator (April 8, 2026) on AI-driven UPF redefinition; Haaretz long-form UPF explainer (May 3, 2026); Brazil/Chile/Mexico octagonal warning labels referenced as the de-facto template.
trends.relevance: Israeli consumers are absorbing the UPF discourse via Haaretz/mainstream press but have no in-app indicator on the foods they log. A simple 'natural / processed / ultra-processed' badge — using a NOVA-style classification on our existing food catalog — is a low-cost trust-builder that aligns with the calmer, more transparent UI direction we identified in W18 and gives Israeli users something MyFitnessPal/Yazio do not localize.

recommendations.what: Ship a MyFitnessPal/Lose It import wizard: user uploads their MFP CSV export (or pastes a Lose It export), our parser maps foreign-language food rows to our Hebrew food catalog (with a fuzzy-match + manual-confirm UI for unmatched items), and the wizard hydrates the user's last 30/90/365 days of diary into our app. Phase 1: MFP CSV only; Phase 2: Lose It; Phase 3: per-meal photo carry-over if available. Pair the launch with a landing page in Hebrew titled 'יבוא יומן MyFitnessPal ב-3 דקות' and a plain-language 'הבטחת יציבות ממשק' (UI-stability promise) for new users.
recommendations.inspired_by: MyFitnessPal April-2026 redesign backlash that bled into May (Piunikaweb May 5, 2026); Lose It macro paywall move; cottage industry of 'best MFP alternative 2026' lists republishing weekly.
recommendations.tier_reason: New feature behind feature flag; new file-upload + mapping UI; new adapter agent (extends Agent 3 Food Resolution with a CSV-to-catalog matcher) and Agent 7 Data Persistence path for backfilled diary entries. No schema change required if mapped imports route through the existing meal-log model. No PII handling beyond what we already collect.
recommendations.why_it_matters: There is a time-bounded acquisition window. The barrier for an MFP user to leave is not feature parity — it is the perceived loss of years of food history. A working CSV import + a public commitment to UI stability turn that lock-in into our advantage. Neo's unit-economics framing: every imported user arrives with a behavioral cohort, which improves our food-catalog hit-rate model and reduces CAC vs. cold-start acquisition. Concrete, measurable, and dated to the MFP news cycle.
recommendations.user_segment: Existing MFP/Lose It users in Israel who are frustrated with the redesign or freemium tightening; Hebrew-first users who tolerated English DBs only because of historical lock-in.

recommendations.what: A 'מצב Wegovy / GLP-1' opt-in profile that adjusts the meal planner and macro targets for GLP-1 users: (a) raises protein floor (≥1.4 g/kg lean mass) to defend muscle mass during rapid weight loss; (b) caps single-meal volumes to satiety-realistic portions; (c) tags foods as 'יכולים להחמיר בחילה' (e.g., high-fat fried, very-sweet, very-cold) when the user is in week 1–2 of a dose escalation; (d) lets users log dose date + injection day so the planner shifts toward bland/low-volume meals on day 1–2 post-injection. No medical advice, no prescription flow — purely a nutrition-context layer. Hebrew copy reviewed by a registered dietitian. Add a one-tap 'יצוא לתזונאי/ת' so users can share the week's meals with their private dietitian.
recommendations.inspired_by: MyFitnessPal Winter-2026 GLP-1 module (now free-tier); Noom Welli's GLP-1-aware coaching; Times of Israel report on Wegovy launch in Israel; ScienceDaily May 2026 GLP-1 side-effect coverage. Adapted to Israel because no incumbent ships this in Hebrew, and the Israeli private-pay cohort is well-defined.
recommendations.tier_reason: New profile flag (additive to user model — no breaking schema change), new behavior in Agent 2 (Nutrition targets) and Agent 5 (Planner), new tag column on food catalog (additive). Feature-flag-able. No payment, no PII expansion, no clinician routing — explicitly NOT a medical/RED feature. Dose-date input is treated like any other diary field.
recommendations.why_it_matters: An Israeli Wegovy user is paying NIS 600–1,300/month out of pocket. They are demonstrably willing to invest in adjacent tools. They have specific, well-documented nutrition needs that a generic macro tracker fails. Building this in Hebrew, integrated with our existing planner, captures a high-LTV cohort that global apps will be slow to localize because their GLP-1 modules are English-first by design. This is also coherent with Q2's emerging direction: 'cohort-aware planner' (last week we identified GLP-1 as a wedge; this week's Wegovy news raises the urgency).
recommendations.user_segment: Israeli adults on Wegovy / Mounjaro / Ozempic (off-label, residual) for weight management; their treating dietitians who currently rely on paper printouts.

recommendations.what: Add a NOVA-style classification field to every catalog food: { 1: 'טבעי / מעובד מינימלית', 2: 'מרכיב מעובד', 3: 'מעובד', 4: 'אולטרה מעובד' }. Display a small octagonal badge inline in the diary and the meal planner — black for NOVA-4, neutral grey for NOVA-1/2 — and surface a weekly 'אחוז אולטרה מעובד מהקלוריות' line on the existing summary screen. Phase 1: classify the top 1,000 most-logged foods manually (with a published rubric). Phase 2: apply a pre-cached classifier to the long tail; show 'לא מסווג עדיין' rather than guessing. Do NOT produce health claims, do NOT block logging, do NOT moralize — purely transparent labeling.
recommendations.inspired_by: Haaretz UPF explainer (May 3, 2026); California AB 1264; bipartisan US UPF labeling bill; FoodNavigator (April 8, 2026) on AI-assisted UPF classification; Brazil/Chile/Mexico octagonal warning system as the visual reference. Adapted from policy to product: while regulators argue, we ship the indicator inside the user's own diary.
recommendations.tier_reason: New additive column on the food catalog (no breaking change to recipe or user models); new UI element on existing screens; no new external API; no PII; feature-flag-able. The risk is editorial (classification correctness, public rubric) rather than technical.
recommendations.why_it_matters: Israeli users are already absorbing the UPF discourse from mainstream press but have nowhere to act on it inside their food log. A non-judgmental indicator with a published rubric builds the trust signal that the calmer-design trend rewards (per W18). It also creates a content moat: a curated Israeli UPF taxonomy across local SKUs (Osem, Strauss, Tnuva, Tara, Telma) is hard for global apps to replicate, and pairs naturally with the W18 'Israeli-product barcode DB' recommendation. Fits the Q2 theme cluster: 'Israeli context + transparent UI'.
recommendations.user_segment: Health-curious Israeli home cooks who follow Haaretz/Ynet health coverage; parents tracking what their household actually eats; users skeptical of fitness-influencer messaging who respond to rubric-based transparency.

process_notes.email_status: NOT SENT — no Gmail/email MCP connector is currently installed in this Cowork session. Recommend installing a Gmail or SMTP connector via search_mcp_registry and re-running, or forwarding the HTML report manually to dviryona8@gmail.com (CC nehoraios08@gmail.com).
process_notes.json_path_note: Wrote to nutrition_research/market_brief.json inside the mounted אפליקציית תזונאי folder because the spec's target path Desktop/approval_version_nutt_app/ is not currently mounted/accessible from this session. Same fallback as W18.
process_notes.theme_continuity: All three recommendations extend Q2 theme clusters established in W17–W18: (1) MFP-import wizard extends 'calmer/stable interaction design'; (2) GLP-1 module is the third consecutive brief carrying GLP-1 forward; (3) UPF badge extends 'Israeli context + transparent UI'. No scattered/random recommendations this fortnight.
process_notes.israeli_context_coverage: 2/3 recommendations are distinctly Israeli-context (GLP-1 Hebrew companion; UPF badge with Israeli SKU rubric). Meets the at-least-1-per-month Israeli-context rule.
